name: Code integrity determined image hash is not valid
definition: Windows Security Event ID 5038 records Code Integrity rejecting a file because its image hash is not valid.
trigger_scenarios: This event is generated by Code Integrity when a file signature is not valid, including unauthorized modification or disk/device error conditions.

key_fields.explanation: The path of the file whose image hash was not valid. Microsoft documents the schema as File Name: %filepath\filename%.
Identifies the host where an unsigned, modified, or corrupt driver or system file attempted to load.
Microsoft notes Code Integrity validates driver or system file integrity each time loaded into memory, and x64 kernel-mode drivers must be digitally signed.

false_positives: Microsoft notes disk device error or file corruption can produce this event.
Broken third-party driver updates can create repeated 5038 events until the vendor package is repaired.
detection_notes: T1553.002 Code Signing subversion is supported when FileName is under C:\Windows\System32\drivers\, C:\Windows\System32\, or a security-product directory and file telemetry shows unauthorized modification before threshold=1 Event 5038. Correlate the file hash and signer with Sysmon Event 6 or EDR data, 5035 driver failure, and vendor update timing; an invalid hash alone can be corruption.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5038
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account
sample_log:
EventID: 5038
Computer: HOST01
File Name: C:\Windows\System32\drivers\bad.sys

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 5038
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5038
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Apply the event-specific field and correlation guidance on this page after retrieving the base events.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 5038
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=5038
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and field aliases vary by Splunk deployment and Windows add-on version.
Map provider fields from the raw event before adding event-specific filtering or aggregation.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 5038
assumptions: The Windows Security channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 5038
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 5038
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 5038
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the field conditions and correlations documented on this page in the destination SIEM.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 5038 mean?
faqs.answer: Event ID 5038 records that Code Integrity found an invalid hash for an image being validated and identifies the file.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 5038 always indicate malware?
faqs.answer: No. File corruption, storage errors, incomplete updates, and third-party software defects can also invalidate a hash or signature.

faqs.question: How should 5038 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Validate FileName, signer and catalog status, file hash, recent writes, update timing, and related driver or service failures before containment.

sources.title: 5038: Microsoft Windows security auditing event reference
